projects.name: Teswiz
projects.short_description: Your Ultimate Open-Source Solution to Automate Real-User Scenarios!
projects.description: Cross-platform testing made easy. Automate your real-user scenarios for Android, iOS, Web, Desktop, API and beyond with one powerful tool.    Teswiz is an open-source test automation framework designed to simplify and streamline the process of testing applications across multiple platforms.    Whether you're working with Android, iOS, web applications, or API, Teswiz provides a unified interface and robust tooling to ensure your tests are reliable, maintainable, and scalable.

form.question: How to support
form.response: Raise a issue in [teswiz](https://github.com/znsio/teswiz) GitHub, or contact [Anand Bagmar](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anandbagmar/) for help or if you face issues using teswiz

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response:
Teswiz is an open-source test automation framework designed to simplify and streamline the process of testing applications across multiple platforms.

Whether you're working with Android, iOS, web applications, or API, Teswiz provides a unified interface and robust tooling to ensure your tests are reliable, maintainable, and scalable.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: It started mainly to avoid reinventing the wheel for myself. In the process, if it can help others, that is even better!

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
What's your open-source villain origin story?
If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
